titlestmt.titleproper: Sumner McKnight Crosby Post Card and Photograph Negative Collection
Sumner McKnight Crosby Post Card and Photograph Negative Collection
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Robert Carlucci

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to <title xlink:type="simple" render="italic">Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS)</title>.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday August 10, 2018 at 18:03
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

did.unittitle: Sumner McKnight Crosby Post Card and Photograph Negative Collection
did.abstract: Collection of post cards mostly of French and English medieval architecture, as well as a variety of other Western European monuments, collected by Sumner McKnight Crosby and donated to the Sterling Memorial Library upon his death in 1982. The collection is boxed as received in 1982 and is divided into three sections: Boxes 1 and 2: post cards arranged alphabetically by building; Boxes 3 and 4: post cards arranged alphabetically by place; Box 5: miscellaneous black and white photographic negatives of medieval architectural monuments. A limited number of post cards contain comments on the reverse.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University. Further use of this material may be subject to the copyright laws of the United States (Title 17, United States Code) or to site license or other rights management terms or conditions.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sumner McKnight Crosby Post Card and Photograph Negative Collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection of post cards mostly of French and English medieval architecture, as well as a variety of other Western European monuments, collected by Sumner McKnight Crosby and donated to the Sterling Memorial Library upon his death in 1982. The collection is boxed as received in 1982 and is divided into three sections: Boxes 1 and 2: post cards arranged alphabetically by building; Boxes 3 and 4: post cards arranged alphabetically by place; Box 5: miscellaneous black and white photographic negatives of medieval architectural monuments. A limited number of post cards contain comments on the reverse.
